summary: A lower court ruled the search warrant that led to the arrest should never have been issued and suppressed the evidence against Crocker. The Appeals Court agreed. Last year the Appeals Court defined that limit as 4 ounces. The Appeals Court also struck down a 1990 voter initiative that criminalized possession of any amount of marijuana. title: Belugas again denied special status
summary: The depleted beluga whales of Cook Inlet won't get special protection under the Alaska Endangered Species Act under a ruling last week by the Alaska Supreme Court. The opinion, issued Friday, capped another skirmish in an ongoing legal battle by conservation and Native groups to force federal and state agencies to take much stronger steps to help the genetically isolated whales recover from a 1990s population crash. A suit to list the whales under the federal Endangered Species Act also had lost in federal court a few years ago. summary: JUNEAU -- Federal officials are proposing to let fish farmers claim Alaska coastal waters and start farming despite a state ban on the practice. summary: Fourth dead bear found in Katmai park The Associated Press ANCHORAGE (August 4, 12:42 pm ADT) - A fourth brown bear has been found dead near a bear-watching stream in Katmai National Park and Preserve. Rangers investigating the illegal shootings of three other bears in the area flew to the site south of Iliamna Lake on Tuesday to look at the cub carcass. title: Pump prices up to $1.93 per gallon
summary: Oil prices inched higher Monday, with Alaska crude crossing back above $40 a barrel for the first time in seven weeks. North Slope crude sold on West Coast open markets closed at $40.09 a barrel, up 39 cents for the day and its highest close since June 1, when it hit its highest price ever at $41.38 a barrel. After falling from recent highs, oil prices have been surging again as traders react to concerns about terrorist attacks, strong demand and relatively tight supplies of gasoline. With higher oil prices, the retail price of gasoline nationally rose for the second week in a row, climbing to nearly $1.93 per gallon, after declining during the five previous weeks, the Energy Department reported Monday. title: Letters to the Editor
summary: Alaska again ranks No. 1 for STD The Associated Press ANCHORAGE (July 6, 10:30 am ADT) - Alaska is ranked highest in the nation for infection from the sexually-transmitted disease chlamydia. The state received reports of 3,900 chlamydia cases in 2003, the most recent year for which information has been compiled, according to the state Section of Epidemiology. More than two-thirds of the cases were in women. It was the third year in a row that Alaska ranked highest. Alaska's rate of infection was 606 cases of chlamydia reported for every 100,000 Alaskans. That's higher than the 591 cases per 100,000 people reported in 2002 and the 437 cases reported in 2001.
